summary: Replaced ftsRowID polynomial hash with FNV-1a 64-bit + NUL separator and added validIdentifierRE check to ResolveByName matchFields.
reason: Polynomial hash had non-trivial collision probability for (scope, id) pairs; on collision DELETE WHERE rowid=? silently evicted an unrelated FTS row. ResolveByName interpolated matchFields into json_extract without the same identifier guard ListField uses, creating a future SQL-injection trap.
validated_outcome: go test ./internal/store passes; defence-in-depth aligns ResolveByName with ListField.
